case_title: J GARCIA CARRION, SA ET AL VS. VINCENT FRIEND ET AL

docket_entries.description: SUMMONS ON COMPLAINT (TRANSACTION ID # 100061423), PROOF OF SERVICE ONLY, FILED BY PLAINTIFF J GARCIA CARRION, SA A CORPORATION ORGANIZED AND EXISTING UNDER THE LAWS OF THE KINGDOM OF SPAIN CIV USA, INC A DELAWARE CORPORATION SERVED JAN-18-2019, PERSONAL SERVICE AS TO DEFENDANT FRIEND WINE MARKETING, INC A CALIFORNIA CORPORATION

REPLY IN SUPPORT OF DEMURRERS TO FIRST AMENDED COMPLAINT (TRANSACTION ID # 63150444) FILED BY DEFENDANT FRIEND, VINCENT AN INDIVIDUAL FRIEND, KATHLEEN AN INDIVIDUAL FRIEND WINE MARKETING, INC A CALIFORNIA CORPORATION CORDELINA WINE COMPANY, LLC A CALIFORNIA LIMITED LIABILITY COMPANY

LAW & MOTION, DEPT. 302, APR-23-2019 HEARING HELD ON DEFENDANTS VINCENT FRIEND, KATHLEEN FRIEND, FRIEND WINE MARKETING, AND CORDELINA WINE COMPANY'S DEMURRER TO THE FIRST AMENDED COMPLAINT. THE COURT ADOPTS THE TENTATIVE RULING. THE DEMURRER IS OVERRULED AS TO THE FIRST AND THIRD CAUSES OF ACTION FOR CONVERSION AND UNFAIR COMPETITION/UNFAIR BUSINESS PRACTICES AND SUSTAINED WITHOUT LEAVE TO AMEND AS TO THE SECOND, FOURTH, AND FIFTH CAUSES OF ACTION FOR BREACH OF CONTRACT, PROMISSORY ESTOPPEL, AND EQUITABLE ESTOPPEL. THE COURT GRANTS DEFENDANTS' REQUEST FOR JUDICIAL NOTICE OF THE UNDERLYING MATERIALS IN THE COURT'S FILE, INCLUDING THE ARBITRATORS' PARTIAL FINAL AWARD AND FINAL AWARD, THIS COURT'S JULY 10, 2018 ORDER GRANTING THE PETITION TO CONFIRM THAT AWARD AND JUDGMENT ENTERED ON THE SAME DATE, AND THIS COURT'S DECEMBER 20, 2018 ORDER SUSTAINING DEFENDANTS' DEMURRER WITHOUT LEAVE TO AMEND AS TO THE PRIOR ACTION BROUGHT AGAINST THEM BY PLAINTIFFS (NO. CGC-18-565128). ORDER IS SIGNED. JUDGE: ETHAN P. SCHULMAN, CLERK: W. TRUPEK, COURT REPORTER: LORETTA NAJERA, CSR# 5153, EMAIL: LNAJERA@SFTC.ORG, REPORTED. (302/EPS)

Notice Of Motion And Motion For An Order For Vincent Friend And Kathleen Friend To Show Cause Why They Should Not Be Held In Contempt Of The Judgment Entered July 10, 2018
Off Calendar Jul-15-2019 Denied
MINI MINUTES: Appearances:
Robert Rivas, Esq. 650-364-8200 of Ropers Majeski Kohn & Bentley for Plaintiff through Court Call.
Mark Schickman, Esq. 415-541-0200 of Freeland Cooper & Foreman for Defendant.

RULING - A R G U E D. The Court indicates it received a late contest. Plaintiff argues. (as fully reported in the reporter's notes/transcript) The Court having reviewed the pleadings and having heard oral argument rules as follows:

PLAINTIFF J GARCIA CARRION, SA, CIV USA, INC.'s Motion For An Order For Vincent Friend And Kathleen Friend To Show Cause Why They Should Not Be Held In Contempt Of The Judgment Entered July 10, 2018.

The Court adopts the tentative ruling. Plaintiffs J. Garcia Carrion, SA (JGC) and CIV USA's motion for an order for Defendants Vincent Friend and Kathleen Friend to show cause why they should not be held in contempt of the judgment entered July 10, 2018 is denied. A determination that a person is guilty of contempt under Code of Civil Procedure sections 1209(a)(5) and 1218 requires a showing "beyond a reasonable doubt." (Mitchell v. Superior Court (1989) 49 Cal.3d 1230, 1256.) A judgment of contempt must meet strict requirements: (1) knowledge of the judgment, (2) ability to comply with it, and (3) willful disobedience. (In re Jones (1975) 47 Cal.App.3d 879, 881.)

The Court cannot conclude beyond a reasonable doubt that Mr. Friend and Ms. Friend willfully disobeyed the July 10, 2018 judgment confirming the Arbitration Award. As an initial matter, Ms. Friend was not even a party to the Award and judgment upon which the contempt request is based, so it is not proper to hold her in contempt for violating that judgment. (Rivas Decl., Ex. 2.) Furthermore, although Defendants previously acknowledged reading the Award as requiring transfer of the civusa.com domain name, these representations do not create a sufficient basis to hold Mr. Friend and Ms. Friend in contempt. The interpretation of the Award became subject to a legal dispute that was not resolved until the Court's April 23, 2019 order regarding the demurrer to JGC's first amended complaint. (Rivas Decl., Ex. 6.) That order concluded that "full rights" to the CIV USA name, as stated in the Award, included the right to the domain name and referenced statements by Defendants' counsel as evidence for reading the Award in this way. (Id.) However, this resolution of the legal dispute regarding the obligations imposed by the Award, even in light of counsel's apparent acquiescence to JGC's reading of the Award, cannot apply retroactively to establish beyond a reasonable doubt that Mr. Friend and Ms. Friend willfully disobeyed the July 10, 2018 judgment. This is especially true given that the judgment merely stated that the Award was confirmed; neither the judgment nor the underlying Award provided an explicit, narrowly-tailored Court directive to transfer the domain name. (See In re Marcus (2006) 138 Cal.App.4th 1009, 1014 ["Any ambiguity in a decree or order must be resolved in favor of an alleged contemnor."]; Wilson v. Superior Court (1987) 194 Cal.App.3d 1259, 1273 ["[P]unishment [for contempt] can only rest upon clear, intentional violation of a specific narrowly drawn order. Specificity is an essential prerequisite of a contempt citation."]; Butler v. Superior Court (1960) 178 Cal.App.2d 763, 765 [a party cannot be held guilty for violating an order that is "uncertain or ambiguous"].)

Order is signed. (see scanned order for details)

Judge: Ethan P. Schulman, Clerk: M. Goodman, Court Reporter: Anthony Vaughn, CSR#6185, tonyvaughn@yahoo.com, Reported. =(302/EPU)

The Court adopts the tentative ruling:

Defendants Vincent Friend, Kathleen Friend, Friend Wine Marketing, and Cordelina Wine Company's demurrer to the first amended complaint is overruled as to the first and third causes of action for conversion and unfair competition/unfair business practices and sustained without leave to amend as to the second, fourth, and fifth causes of action for breach of contract, promissory estoppel, and equitable estoppel. The Court grants Defendants' request for judicial notice of the underlying materials in the Court's file, including the arbitrators' partial final award and final award, this Court's July 10, 2018 order granting the petition to confirm that award and judgment entered on the same date, and this Court's December 20, 2018 order sustaining Defendants' demurrer without leave to amend as to the prior action brought against them by Plaintiffs (No. CGC-18-565128).
